1.description: The Wall/Main Street disconnect and the subsequent reconnect. The blue line in this figureآ shows the series for the S&P 500 index over potential GDP, scaled to equal one on December 31, 2019. The red line shows the series for the GDP over potential GDP. Potential GDP is the estimate by the Congressional Budget Office (CBO). For data sources, see theInternet Appendix.

2.description: Asset price overshooting. This figureآ shows simulations of the equilibrium with low aversion to overshootingدˆ=0.1$\protect \psi =0.1$(solid lines) and high aversionدˆ=âˆ‍$\protect \psi =\infty$(dashed lines). We set the adjustment rateخ¸$\theta$equal to 0.5, the discount rateدپ$\rho$equal to 0.04, and the parameters of the adjusting stockholderm,n$m,n$equal to 0.7 and 0.1, respectively.

3.description: The role of inertia in driving asset price overshooting. This figureآ shows simulations of the equilibrium with high inertia and low adjustment rateخ¸=0.5$\protect \theta =0.5$(solid lines) and low inertia and with high adjustment rateخ¸=5$\protect \theta =5$(dashed lines). The dotted lines correspond to the potential levels for the corresponding variables. We use the same parameters as in the overshooting case of Figure2.

4.description: Overshooting with an interest rate lower bound. The left panel of this figureآ plots the asset price function with an interest rate lower bound (solid curve) and without a lower bound (dashed line). The right panel decomposes the asset price (solid curve) into components explained by future interest rates (dashed curve) and future cash flows (dotted line). We set the boundrآ¯$\overline{r}$equal toâˆ’0.5%$-0.5\%$and use the same parameters as in the overshooting case of Figure2.

5.description: Equilibrium dynamics with an interest rate lower bound. This figureآ shows simulations of the equilibrium over time with an interest rate lower bound (solid lines) and no lower bound (dotted lines). We set the boundrآ¯$\overline{r}$equal toâˆ’0.5%$-0.5\%$, and we use the same parameters as in the overshooting case of Figure2.

6.description: Asset price overshooting during the COVID-19 recovery. This figureآ shows the evolution of the log price of the market bond portfolio,pMBt$p^{MB}\left(t\right)$, along with the log S&P 500 index, log house price index, and log household net worth normalized by potential output according to the CBO. All series are normalized to zero on December 31, 2019.

7.description: Drivers of the market bond portfolio in the COVID-19 recovery. This figureآ shows one-year TIPS forward rates at select horizons.
